expressions.english: to cry all night long
expressions.notes:
밤 = night
밤새 = overnight; all night long

expressions.english: to cry out of joy
expressions.notes:
기뻐다 = to be joyful, glad
-아/어/여서 = because, since

expressions.english: to cry out loud
expressions.notes:
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud
큰 is the adjective form of 크다.
소리 = sound
큰 소리 = loud noise, loud sound

expressions.english: Don't cry.
expressions.notes: -지 마세요 = (polite) Don't + V

expressions.english: Stop crying.
expressions.notes: 그만 -아/어/여요. = Stop + V-ing.; Now, let's just + V.

expressions.english: Why are you crying?
expressions.notes:
왜 = why; for what reason; how come
-고 있다 = to be + V-ing

expressions.english: I wanted to cry, but I held back.
expressions.notes:
-고 싶다 = to want to + V
-지만 = but, however; though
참다 = to suppress, to hold back

expressions.english: She is pretty even when she cries.
expressions.notes:
그 = the; that
여자 = girl, woman
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
-도 = too, also
예쁘다 = to be pretty

expressions.english: I was so sad that I cried all night long.
expressions.notes:
너무 = too much, excessively; very
슬프다 = to be sad

expressions.english: I cried so much that my eyes got swollen.
expressions.notes:
눈 = eye
붓다 = to swell

expressions.english: He tends to cry easily.
expressions.notes:
사람 = person, people
잘 = well; carefully
-(으)ㄴ 편이다 = to tend to + V

expressions.english: The baby cried himself to sleep.
expressions.notes:
아기 = baby
-다 지치다 = to get tired from + V-ing; to get tired while + V-ing
잠 = sleep
잠이 들다 = to fall asleep

expressions.english: It seems like the baby is crying because she's hungry.
expressions.notes:
배고프다 = to be hungry, starving
-것 같다 = It seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.english: I was so happy to receive the award that I cried.
expressions.notes:
상 = prize
받다 = to receive

expressions.english: Stop crying and explain it to me in detail.
expressions.notes:
자세하다 = to be specific, detailed
자세히 is an adverb form of 자세하다.
설명하다 = to explain
-아/어/여 보다 = (polite) imperative
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.english: A kid fell down and is crying.
expressions.notes:
어리다 = to be young
아린 is the adjective form of 어리다.
아이 = child, kid; baby
넘어지다 = to fall (down); to trip

expressions.english: I cried my eyes out, and now I feel better.
expressions.notes:
심킷 = as much as one wishes, to one's heart's content
-더니 = S + V-ed and and as a result
기분 = feelings; mood
좀 = a little; please
나아지다 = to get better; to improve

expressions.korean: 어제 슬픈 영화를 보고 많이 울었어요.
expressions.english: Yesterday, I watched a sad movie and cried a lot.
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read
슬픈 is the adjective form of 슬프다.
영화 = movie
많이 = a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.

expressions.korean: 울지만 말고 무슨 일이 있었는지 말을 해 봐요.
expressions.english: Don't cry, just tell me what happened.
expressions.notes:
무슨 = what; what kind of
일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion
있다 = something happened, something came up
-기만 하다 = just + V, to only + V
문제 = problem, issue; thing; matter
해결되다 = to be solved
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.korean: 울기만 하다고 문제가 해결되는 건 아니잖아요.
expressions.english: Crying doesn't solve the problem.
expressions.notes:
-기만 하다 = just + V, to only + V
문제 = problem, issue; thing; matter
해결되다 = to be solved
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative
-(잖아요) is a sentence ending used most commonly when correcting another person's remark or when refreshing someone's memory.
